semanticCore: Transgression, exceeding limits, flooding, or tyranny
quranicRelevance: Exceeding moral bounds/tyranny (Tughyan), Pharaoh as a tyrant ('tagha'), flooding of water ('tagha al-ma''), and false deities (Taghoot).

keyDerivations.meaning: To transgress, exceed bounds, overflow, or tyrannize
False deities, idols, Satan, or tyrants (who exceed bounds)

figurativeOrRareUsages: لَمَّا طَغَى المَاءُ (69:11): When the water rose high / overflowed (the Flood).
discardedNoise: Specific details of pre-Islamic idol temples.
Obscure details of river silt deposits.
summaryEn: The root denotes crossing boundaries, capturing human pride and tyranny (Tughyan), false gods (Taghoot), and overflowing natural disasters.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على مجاوزة الحدود، ممثلاً الاستكبار البشري والطغيان (طغيان)، والشرك والأوثان (طاغوت)، والكوارث الطبيعية العارمة.
rawText: طغى 1 , (As, O, K,) [aor., app., ﹻ,] inf. n. طَفٌّ, (TK, [or, accord. to Freytag طُفُوفٌ, which see in what follows,]) It (a thing) was, or became, near. (As, O, K.) You say, أَخَذْتُ مِنْ مَتَاعِى مَا خَفَّ وَطَفَّ i. e. [I took, of my goods,] what [was light, and] was near to me. (As, O.) And طَفَّ الشَّىْءُ مِنَ الشَّىْءِ The thing was, or became, near to the thing. (O, K. *) And خُذْ مَا طَفَّ لَكَ, and 🡻ٱسْتَطَفَّلَكَ, (S, Meyd, O, K,) and 🡻أَطَفَّلَكَ, (Meyd, O, TA,) Take thou what has risen to thee, and become within thy power or reach, (S, O, K, TA,) and become attainable [to thee], or prepared [for thee], (AZ, Meyd, TA,) and become near to thee: (K, TA:) or what has risen to view, and has appeared, [to thee,] to be taken: (TA:) [for] طَفَّ, inf. n. طُفُوفٌ, signifies it rose [app. so as to become visible]: and it was, or became, little in quantity: the saying is a prov., relating to a man's being content with a part of that which he wants: (Meyd:) and in like manner one says, خُذْ مَا دَفَّ لَكَ, and ٱسْتَدَفَّ: (AZ, Meyd, TA:) and Ks mentions, in relation to a man's being content with a part of that which he wants, the saying, خُذْ مَا طَفَّ لَكَ وَدَعْ مَا 🡻ٱسْتَطَفَّ لَكَ [app. meaning, if the saying be correctly thus related, Take what is within thy power, or reach, and leave what has risen to thy view so as to invite approach]; i. e. be content with what is within thy power. (TA.) === طَفَّتِ الشَّمْسُ: === and طَفَّ said of a bird: see 2. === مَرَّيَطفُّ [app. يَطِفٌّ] He passed by hastening, or going quickly. (O.) ___ طَفَّ النَّاقَةَ, (O, K,) aor. ﹹ, (O, TA,) inf. n. طَفٌّ, (TA,) He (a man, O) bound the legs of the she-camel, (O, K,) all of them. (O.) === طَفَّهُ He (a man, O) raised it (i. e. a thing, O) with his leg or foot, or with his arm or hand. (O, K.) And طَفَّ بِفُلَانٍ مَوْضِعَ كَذَا He raised such a one to such a place; and made him to be on a level with it. (TA.) === And طَفَّ الحَائِطَ, inf. n. طَفٌّ, He mounted upon the wall. (TA.)
